title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
summary: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

title: Trump Tells Congress Why He Doesn’t Need Its Authorization for the Iran War
summary: In letters to the House and Senate, the president asserted that the hostilities had “terminated,” in an apparent attempt to avoid having to seek congressional approval.

title: Trump Says He’s ‘Not Satisfied’ With Iran’s Latest Proposal
summary: Also, Spirit Airlines prepares to shut down. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

title: Federal Appeals Court Temporarily Halts Abortion Pill Access by Mail
summary: The court order, in a lawsuit by the state of Louisiana, pauses a Food and Drug Administration regulation that greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
summary: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Congress’s refusal to extend federal tax credits.

title: F.D.A. Grants Early Access to Promising Drug for Pancreatic Cancer
summary: Patients with one of the deadliest cancers have been pleading for an unapproved treatment that may prolong their lives.

title: Eric Swalwell Was Proficient in Social Media. So Were His Accusers.
summary: Eric Swalwell used social media to boost his image and meet women, accusers said. Some of those same women used apps and influencers to join forces years later.

title: After Prison, a Financial Titan Plots an Unlikely Comeback
summary: Bill McGlashan served time for trying to buy his son’s way into college during the Varsity Blues scandal. He hopes his new venture will restore his name — and save the planet.

title: Bard College’s President, Leon Botstein, Will Retire After Epstein Revelations
summary: The president, Leon Botstein, who had run Bard for 50 years, faced scrutiny over his connections to Jeffrey Epstein.

title: Authorities Share New Video of Suspect in White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
summary: The F.B.I. and prosecutors shared an annotated video showing the suspect sprinting through a checkpoint and raising a gun. A frame-by-frame analysis suggests that he may have fired.

title: N.I.H. Reinstates Employee Put on Leave After Criticizing Trump Research Cuts
summary: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.

title: Kennedy Messaged With Scandal P.R. Firm While Seeking Cabinet Nomination
summary: An associate of the firm, which has been accused of running a smear campaign against the actress Blake Lively, promised to suppress negative stories about Robert F. Kennedy Jr., records show.

title: Federal Judge Blocks Plan to End Deportation Protections for Yemenis
summary: A Supreme Court court decision involving similar deportation protections for Haitians and Syrians could have implications for Yemeni migrants.

title: Spirit Airlines Prepares to Shut Down After Trump Administration Bailout Falls Through
summary: The low-fare airline, which has struggled for years, had been trying to secure a $500 million lifeline from the Trump administration.

title: Spirit Airlines May Be Shutting Down. Here’s What to Do if You’re Scheduled to Fly.
summary: After two stints in bankruptcy, the low-cost airline is set to shut down early Saturday. Here’s what travelers should know about rebooking and refunds.

title: Stocks and Oil Prices Sent Conflicting Signals in April Amid Havoc of Iran War
summary: The S&P 500 notched five weeks of gains for the first time in roughly 18 months. In April, it rose more than 10 percent, its best month since November 2020.

title: Voters Sue Over Louisiana Governor’s Move to Delay Primary
summary: Legal challenges are mounting over the decision to suspend the state’s May House primary after a Supreme Court ruling found that the congressional map was unconstitutional.

title: Florida Redistricts in Republicans’ Favor
summary: Our politics reporter Nick Corasaniti explains how Florida redrew its congressional district maps to create four more Republican-leaning House seats.

title: Southern States Move to Redistrict Under Weakened Voting Rights Act
summary: Republican-led legislatures in Tennessee and Alabama will reconvene in the coming days. Unlike in Tennessee, however, a new map in Alabama will require Supreme Court action.

title: Georgia Governor Sets Date for Special Election to Fill David Scott’s House Seat
summary: The winner will serve out the rest of Representative David Scott’s seat in Congress after the longtime lawmaker’s death last month.

title: On May Day Cuba’s Communist Leaders Deliver a Defiant Tone to the U.S.
summary: In front of the American Embassy in Havana, the Cuban government held its annual International Worker’s Day celebration as the United States sought to end the communist ruling system.

title: May Day Protests Pushing for Worker Protections Expected Across the U.S.
summary: Organizers have billed the events as a nationwide day of action to demand policies that put the interests of working people over those of the ultrawealthy.

title: 6 Common Medications That May Decrease Dementia Risk
summary: Some vaccines, along with heart medications and other drugs, appear to have a protective benefit.

title: 4 Medications That May Increase Dementia Risk
summary: Experts have flagged several over-the-counter and prescription medications.

title: Can the Costume Institute Survive Without the Met Gala?
summary: The Metropolitan Museum of Art and the party that has supported its fashion wing may be entering a new era.

title: With ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ and the Met Gala, Fashion Fans May Get Déjà Vu
summary: That’s “meta” with a lowercase m, by the way. After a weekslong promotional blitz for “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” the scene on Monday may feel very familiar.

title: Beyoncé’s Best Met Gala Looks as She Returns to the Event After 10 Years
summary: After 10 years away from the event, the superstar will return as a co-chair on Monday. Here’s what she has worn to her seven past gala appearances.

title: This Is What’s Behind Trump’s Relentlessness
summary: “Animal spirits” and American presidents.

title: He Signed Away His Right to Sue by Subscribing to Disney+
summary: Thanks to a series of Supreme Court decisions, nearly 80 percent of Fortune 500 companies can — and do — use forced arbitration with consumers or workers.

title: FEMA Disaster Aid is Flowing Slowly In Trump’s Second Term
summary: During President Trump’s second term, the disaster declarations that unlock money are taking longer than in the past. Blue states wait the longest and they hear ‘no’ more often.

title: Mother Runs for Office in India After Daughter Was Raped and Killed
summary: Ratna Debnath is a candidate for Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party, which is campaigning on women’s safety in an Indian state election.

title: Cornell President’s Car Bumps Into Students After Confrontation Over Gaza
summary: After a debate over the war, students say the university president hit them with his vehicle. He says he was the victim in the incident.

title: U.S. Indictment of Sinaloa’s Governor Is a Reckoning for Residents of the Mexican State
summary: The U.S. indictment of a Mexican governor confirms what many residents say they had long suspected: The line between organized crime and the upper echelons of government has blurred.
